highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.139: Agent View & /goal Command (Research Preview)
highlights.summary: This release introduces the agent view — a unified list of all your Claude Code sessions (running, blocked, or done) — accessible via `claude agents`. It also adds the `/goal` command, which lets you set a completion condition so Claude keeps working autonomously across multiple turns until the goal is met.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.142: New Agent Flags & Opus 4.7 as Fast Mode Default
highlights.summary: This release adds powerful new flags to `claude agents` — including `--model`, `--effort`, `--permission-mode`, and `--mcp-config` — giving you fine-grained control over background sessions. Fast mode now defaults to Opus 4.7, meaning quicker tasks will automatically use the latest and most capable model.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.143: Plugin Dependency Enforcement & Context Cost Projection
highlights.summary: You can no longer accidentally break your plugin setup — `claude plugin disable` now blocks you if another enabled plugin depends on the target, and provides a ready-to-paste disable-chain command. A new projected context cost feature also helps you monitor per-turn and session-level token spend before it gets expensive.

highlights.title: Introducing Claude Opus 4.7
highlights.summary: Anthropic has officially announced Claude Opus 4.7, the model now powering Claude Code's fast mode by default. This post covers the model's new capabilities and how it fits into the broader Claude model lineup, making it essential reading for developers who want to understand what's running under the hood.

highlights.title: How Claude Code Works in Large Codebases
highlights.summary: This Anthropic blog post shares best practices for using Claude Code effectively when your project is large and complex, including where to start and how to structure your workflow. It's a practical guide for developers who've outgrown simple single-file tasks and want to scale up their AI-assisted development.

highlights.title: Using Claude Code Agent in VS Code with DeepSeek v4 Model
highlights.summary: This Bilibili video walks through how to configure Claude Code inside VS Code to use an alternative model (DeepSeek v4) by setting `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL` and `ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN` in your `settings.json`. It's a handy guide for developers in regions where direct Anthropic API access is difficult or expensive.

highlights.title: Claude Code Tutorial: Beginner to Advanced in 20 Minutes
highlights.summary: This YouTube tutorial covers Claude Code from the ground up to more advanced usage patterns in a compact 20-minute format. It's an efficient starting point for developers who want a structured overview without committing to a multi-hour course.

highlights.title: 9-Minute Guide: Claude Code Installation, How It Works & Real Usage (China Direct Connection)
highlights.summary: This Bilibili video offers a fast, beginner-friendly walkthrough of installing Claude Code, understanding its core principles, and seeing it in real use — with a focus on accessibility for users in mainland China. If you've been struggling to get Claude Code set up, this under-10-minute guide is a great first stop.

tips_of_the_day: Use the new `/goal` command to define a clear end condition before starting a long autonomous task — Claude will keep iterating until the goal is met, saving you from repeatedly re-prompting.
Before disabling a plugin, run `claude plugin list` to check for dependents first. With v2.1.143's dependency enforcement, you'll get a ready-to-paste disable-chain command if anything blocks you.

If you're working on a large codebase, start by giving Claude Code a high-level map of your project structure in your first message — pointing it to key entry files and config locations helps it navigate faster and reduces wasted context.
